title: Infants with Jaundice: 7 Natural Herbal Teas

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of jaundice. This is due to its active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and ease liver discomfort. The tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may also help improve liver function and reduce bilirubin levels, a key factor in jaundice.  If you're considering trying ginger tea for jaundice, start by consulting a healthcare professional for advice. They may recommend brewing 1-2 teaspoons of fresh ginger in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and drink it 2-3 times a day. You can also add honey to make it more palatable. As you take ginger tea, you may start to notice improvements in your symptoms. The tea may help reduce nausea and vomiting, and alleviate fatigue and weakness. Your skin may also appear less yellow, and your eyes may regain their normal color. However, if your symptoms worsen or you experience severe abdominal pain, dark urine, or pale stools, seek medical attention immediately.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, also known as lemongrass tea, has been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of jaundice. The tea contains compounds like citral, geraniol, and limonene, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties may help reduce liver inflammation and promote the removal of bilirubin, a pigment that causes jaundice.  To try Cymbopogon citratus tea for jaundice, start by drinking 1-2 cups of the tea per day. You can make the tea by steeping 1-2 teaspoons of dried lemongrass in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and drink it after it has cooled down.  If you experience any improvement in your symptoms, it is likely due to the tea's ability to reduce liver inflammation and promote the removal of bilirubin. You may notice a decrease in yellowing of the skin and eyes, and an increase in appetite. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before making any changes to your treatment plan.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal teas for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium teas have been traditionally used to help alleviate jaundice symptoms. This is because the plant contains bioactive compounds like thujone, artabsin, and artesenin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help reduce liver inflammation and promote liver function, which is crucial in treating jaundice. When using Artemisia absinthium teas for jaundice, it's essential to consult a healthcare professional for guidance. They may recommend brewing 1-2 teaspoons of dried Artemisia absinthium leaves in boiling water for 5-10 minutes. The tea should be consumed 2-3 times a day, ideally between meals. As the liver begins to function normally, you may notice a reduction in symptoms like yellowing of the skin and eyes, dark urine, and pale stools. Additionally, some people may experience an improvement in energy levels and a decrease in nausea. However, it's crucial to monitor your condition closely and adjust your treatment plan as needed.
remedies.image_alt: Artemisia absinthium herbal teas for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been used to help alleviate jaundice, a condition characterized by a yellowing of the skin and eyes due to liver dysfunction. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain properties and constituents that contribute to its potential benefits, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide. These compounds may help improve liver function by increasing blood flow and reducing inflammation, thus allowing the liver to work more efficiently. Flavonoids, in particular, have been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the liver cells from damage. If you're considering using Ginkgo biloba teas for jaundice, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before taking any action. They can provide guidance on the safe dosage and potential interactions with other medications. In general, it's recommended to steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves in hot water for 5-10 minutes. Drink 2-3 cups a day, ideally between meals, to allow for optimal absorption. Monitor your symptoms closely, as improvements may be noticed within a few days. However, it's crucial to note that Ginkgo biloba teas are not a substitute for medical treatment. Jaundice can be a sign of a more serious underlying condition, and if you experience severe symptoms, such as vomiting, diarrhea, or abdominal pain, seek medical attention immediately. Regular blood tests may be necessary to track your liver function and adjust your treatment plan accordingly.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle tea, may help with jaundice due to its ability to protect and regenerate liver cells. The tea contains compounds like silymarin, a flavonoid complex that has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce liver damage and promote healing. Silymarin also increases the production of liver enzymes that help remove toxins from the body.  If you or someone you know is experiencing jaundice, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. However, if advised to try Silybum marianum tea, here are some steps to consider:  First, find a reputable supplier of high-quality Silybum marianum tea leaves or extract. Follow the recommended brewing instructions to make a tea that is strong enough to be effective but not so strong that it causes stomach upset. Typically, a strong tea is made by steeping 1-2 teaspoons of dried Silybum marianum leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes. You can drink 2-3 cups of this tea per day as directed by your healthcare professional. When taking Silybum marianum tea, you may experience some improvements in your condition, such as a reduction in jaundice symptoms, a decrease in fatigue, and an improvement in appetite. You may also notice some side effects, like stomach upset or diarrhea, but these are typically mild and temporary. However, if you experience severe symptoms or if your condition worsens, seek medical attention immediately. Regular monitoring by a healthcare professional is crucial to ensure the tea is not causing any interactions with other medications or exacerbating the underlying condition.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal teas for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of jaundice, a condition characterized by a buildup of bilirubin in the blood, causing yellowing of the skin and eyes. The tea's active compounds, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, are believed to contribute to its potential benefits. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with jaundice. Flavonoids, on the other hand, have been shown to possess hepatoprotective effects, potentially protecting the liver from damage and promoting its function.  If you're considering using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for jaundice, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before making any decisions. In general, you can try drinking 1-2 cups of the tea per day, made by steeping 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. However, it's crucial to monitor your symptoms and adjust the dosage accordingly.  Taking action may lead to improved liver function, reduced bilirubin levels, and alleviation of jaundice symptoms. However, it's also possible that the tea may not be effective in all cases, and jaundice symptoms may persist. In some instances, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may interact with medications or worsen underlying conditions, so it's crucial to work closely with a healthcare professional to avoid any adverse effects.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita teas have been traditionally used to help with jaundice in infants and children. The main reason for this is the presence of apigenin and luteolin, two flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling in the liver and gallbladder, allowing them to work more efficiently and removing bilirubin, the pigment responsible for jaundice. Chamomilla recutita teas also contain bisabolol, an anti-inflammatory sesquiterpene, which helps to soothe and calm the digestive system. If you're considering using Chamomilla recutita teas for jaundice, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional first, especially if your child is under six months old or has a severe case of jaundice. To use Chamomilla recutita teas, you can make a tea by steeping one teaspoon of dried flowers in a cup of boiling water for five to ten minutes. Strain the tea and give it to your child in small amounts, ideally after feeding. You can also mix it with breast milk or formula to make it more palatable. When using Chamomilla recutita teas for jaundice, you can expect to see improvements in your child's condition within a few days. The tea may help to reduce the yellowish discoloration of the skin and eyes, and your child may become more energetic and playful. In some cases, the tea may also help to regulate bowel movements and prevent constipation, which can contribute to jaundice. However, it's crucial to note that Chamomilla recutita teas are not a substitute for medical treatment. If your child's jaundice persists or worsens, you should seek medical attention immediately.
remedies.image_alt: Chamomilla recutita herbal teas for jaundice

intro_desc: Jaundice is a common condition in newborn babies, where the skin and eyes turn yellow due to a buildup of bilirubin in the blood. This happens when the liver can't remove bilirubin from the blood quickly enough. If left untreated, high levels of bilirubin can cause serious brain damage and other complications. Jaundice can make babies look and feel sick, and it can be painful for them to eat and breastfeed. They might be fussy, sleepy, and have trouble feeding, which can be stressful for both the baby and the parents. If a baby's bilirubin levels get too high, they might need to stay in the hospital for treatment, which can be scary and overwhelming for everyone involved.  In some cases, hospitals may prescribe herbal teas to help treat jaundice in newborns. These teas work by gently stimulating the liver to remove bilirubin from the blood. They are usually given to the mother to drink, so the baby can benefit from the medicinal properties while still breastfeeding. The teas help to stimulate the liver and improve the flow of bile, which helps to remove bilirubin from the blood more efficiently. This can help reduce the baby's bilirubin levels and prevent the need for hospital treatment.
